page: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Its multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only bandwidth for both small and large blocks, alongside strong large-block write bandwidth. This memory performance supports high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by top-tier results in PassMark database benchmarks and Redis SET operations, which exceed 218 million operations per second.

The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate cryptographic, integer, and machine learning workloads. Consequently, the instance achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale databases, real-time analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the r8i.32xlarge achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% for database operations, Redis SET transactions, and multi-threaded decompression. Equipped with Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), it also delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language model inference. This instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large relational databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 1 TB of DDR5 RAM for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
1024.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 8.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated machine learning and cryptography.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
